message:
perf(storage): skip no-op writes on metadata upserts + export-hash updates

Two write-elision optimisations in the sqlite storage layer, both
wired together in the auto-flush path so the round-2
byte-identical-JSONL fast path (commit 281269e) extends all the
way to the WAL page level.

1. upsert_metadata_key_in_tx now filters its UPDATE with
   `AND value != ?`. If the row exists and already holds the
   requested value, the UPDATE matches zero rows and returns 0 —
   exactly the same row count an absent row returns. To tell the
   two cases apart without a second SELECT, the `updated == 0`
   branch now consults a cheap `Self::metadata_equals(conn, key,
   value)` helper before falling through to INSERT. Net effect:
   setting METADATA_JSONL_CONTENT_HASH /
   METADATA_JSONL_OBSERVED_SIZE / similar scalars to a value
   that's already on disk performs zero WAL writes instead of a
   noop UPDATE that still dirties the page.

2. New pub(crate) set_changed_export_hashes_in_tx(exports) method
   on SqliteStorage, parallel in shape to set_export_hashes_in_tx
   but with a per-entry guard: before the DELETE + INSERT pair
   runs for each (issue_id, content_hash), it calls
   get_export_hash(issue_id) and short-circuits if the existing
   hash matches — returning a count of rows that actually changed
   rather than rows touched. Dedup via the existing
   dedupe_export_hash_batch() helper, so duplicate IDs in the
   input collapse to one row.

   Before: finalizing an auto-flush with N dirty issues always
   issued 2N row operations (DELETE + INSERT) plus a WAL-dirty
   timestamp update per row, even for the common case where the
   content_hash was byte-identical to what was already stored
   (e.g. a dirty marker that didn't change content).
   After: rows whose hash is already current are skipped entirely;
   only genuinely-changed hashes pay the DELETE + INSERT.

   New unit test test_set_changed_export_hashes_skips_unchanged_rows
   pins the contract: the first call with the current hash returns
   0 and leaves exported_at untouched; a follow-up call with a new
   hash returns 1 and mutates the row.

3. src/sync/mod.rs::finalize_incremental_auto_flush swaps the
   unconditional set_export_hashes_in_tx(issue_hashes) call for
   set_changed_export_hashes_in_tx, so the byte-identical
   auto-flush path (which also short-circuits the JSONL rewrite)
   now also avoids touching export_hashes rows that haven't moved.
   A change-then-revert workflow that lands back on the original
   content will observe zero WAL pages dirtied in the export-hash
   table, matching the zero-write JSONL fast path.

No external API breakage: set_export_hashes_in_tx is retained for
callers that legitimately need the unconditional-overwrite
behaviour (e.g. recovery / rebuild paths). The new method is
pub(crate) since it's only reachable through the sync module.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
